feed.title: Daily Digest
feed.subtitle: Quote, fact, life hack, fortune cookie, and headlines of the day

entry.title: Fortune Cookie of the Day
entry.content:
Always do right. This will gratify some people and astonish the rest.
Lucky Numbers: 80, 71, 9, 99, 94, 26

entry.title: Life Hack of the Day
entry.content: To clean your shower head. Tie a bag of vinegar around your shower head and leave it overnight.

entry.title: Useless Fact of the Day
entry.content: In 1933, Mickey Mouse, an animated cartoon character, received 800,000 fan letters.

entry.title: Quote of the Day — Helen Keller
entry.content: “The only thing worse than being blind is having sight but no vision.” — Helen Keller

entry.title: Mexico City police teargas teachers’ protest 10 days before World Cup
entry.content: Teachers associated with CNTE union were marching toward Zócalo for salary raises and reversal of pension laws Riot police fired teargas at teachers who were marching toward Mexico City’s historic Zócalo plaza, just days before the square is expected to host the 2026 World Cup “Fan Fest”. The incident is the second time police have clashed with teachers in the past week, and more conflict is likely as Mexico City prepares to hold the opening game of the Fifa World Cup on 11 June.“This event will have to be suspended,” Filiberto Frausto, a union leader, told AFP, which witnessed police firing teargas on 1 June. “A cause like ours should be far above – it’s far more important than a little bit of distraction and fun.” Continue reading...

entry.title: Alphabet plans to raise $80 billion to pay for AI buildout
entry.content: The Google parent company plans to raise the funds by selling stock.

entry.title: Defense tech darling Mach Industries hits $1.8B valuation, a 4x jump in a year
entry.content: In a wild ride for 22-year-old founder and CEO Ethan Thornton, Mach Industries has raised another $300 million. It already has five autonomous vehicles in development and completed a major acquisition.

entry.title: Nvidia chases $200B CPU market with AI agent PCs from Microsoft, Dell, and HP
entry.content: If Nvidia has cracked a way to bring AI agents easily, safely, and usefully to the masses, it could — and should — be big.

entry.title: Anthropic races toward a Wall Street debut with a confidential SEC filing
entry.content: Artificial intelligence company Anthropic is moving toward going public on Wall Street, the latest chapter in its meteoric rise from a little-known research laboratory to one of the leading AI companies valued at $965 billion.

entry.title: Ronald LaPread, Commodores' co-founder and bassist, dies at 75
entry.content: Ronald LaPread, bassist on Commodores songs like 'Easy,' 'Three Times a Lady' and 'Brick House,' has died. He was 75.

entry.title: From the stage to the future: Where are Startup Battlefield’s alumni now?
entry.content: We wanted to show you what happens after the confetti falls. We checked in with some of our recent alumni, many of whom have sat down with us on Build Mode: The Founder Survival Guide, TechCrunch's podcast for founders at every stage.

entry.title: 'Euphoria' has ended after three seasons. Why Sam Levinson wanted to honor Angus Cloud with the finale
entry.content: 'Euphoria' is officially over after three seasons. 'It's a tragic one in the end, but it's the truth,' creator Sam Levinson said of the series finale.

entry.title: Why Dogstar's comeback is still comeback-ing
entry.content: Keanu Reeves' L.A.-based alt-rock band reunited for a 2023 album. Now it's just released another.

entry.title: Florida sues OpenAI, Sam Altman, in first-of-its-kind lawsuit over violent incidents
entry.content: The lawsuit partially revolves around a shooting at Florida State University last year, and ChatGPT's alleged role in the incident.

entry.title: '60 Minutes' veteran Scott Pelley says CBS News bosses are 'murdering' the program
entry.content: Pelley also attacked the credentials of Nick Bilton, a former New York Times tech reporter and documentary filmmaker who has never run a TV news operation.

entry.title: Florida sues OpenAI and CEO Sam Altman; AG says company concealed serious risks of ChatGPT
entry.content: The state of Florida filed a lawsuit against OpenAI and CEO Sam Altman on Monday, claiming the company knowingly released and aggressively marketed ChatGPT to the public while concealing serious risks.

entry.title: Hackers hijacked Instagram accounts by tricking Meta AI support chatbot into granting access
entry.content: Several users on social media reported having their Instagram accounts hacked over the weekend. Meta's own support chatbot was blamed for allowing hackers to hijack accounts.

entry.title: 10 minutes backstage with Rostam at the Ford
entry.content: After a show in Los Angeles, musician and former Vampire Weekend member chats about some of his LP's themes and inspirations (and also Coldplay).

entry.title: Water access is now a risk factor in SpaceX’s IPO
entry.content: The company says it needs "significant" water resources to cool its data centers, and that access to abundant, affordable water is a challenge.

entry.title: I do-a! Dua Lipa and Callum Turner marry in London town hall ceremony
entry.content: Dua Lipa and Callum Turner tied the knot over the weekend in London. They were seen hand-in-hand leaving the historic Old Marylebone Town Hall in Westminster.

entry.title: In 'Not Suitable for Work,' Mindy Kaling couples rom-com antics with professional ambition
entry.content: Mindy Kaling's latest comedy series features an ensemble cast of 20-somethings in New York, centering on roommates Abby (Avantika) and AJ (Ella Hunt) as they navigate love and work.

entry.title: How to watch Sony's State of Play showcase on June 2 at 5PM ET
entry.content: Sony will deliver more than an hour of trailers and game news this week.

entry.title: A new app, The Mall, is building a universal feed for online shopping
entry.content: The Mall lets shoppers build a personalized feed of brands, track sales and drops, and discover products across thousands of retailers.

entry.title: Cancer is now a story of the good, the bad and the ugly – but also hope | Devi Sridhar
entry.content: It’s natural to focus on breakthroughs, but there are many challenges in Britain and around the world. There is no magic bullet, but there’s room for optimismCancer causes nearly one in six deaths worldwide every year, some 10 million all told. That is a stunning number, but it also masks the reality that some cancers are more deadly than others. We have become remarkably good at detecting and treating melanoma and prostate cancer, for example, and today five-year survival rates for those cancers are well over 90% in most rich countries. Others, such as pancreatic cancer, are more difficult. In the UK, just over one in 20 people with pancreatic cancer are still alive five years after diagnosis.That is why a new drug for pancreatic cancer, called daraxonrasib and announced at the American Society of Clinical Oncology’s (Asco) annual meeting in Chicago at the weekend, has been met with such jubilation. The drug – taken as a pill once a day – doubled the survival time of those enrolled in a 500-person trial, with fewer side effects compared to traditional chemotherapy. The drug works by shutting down a protein, Kras, that causes cancer cells to grow and divide. One longtime cancer researcher reported that she cried reading the results. With so few effective treatments for this cancer available, the drug is likely to be a real game-changer. Continue reading...

entry.title: Soccer Glory in France Spurs Violence Again—and Political Backlash
entry.content: President Emmanuel Macron says the country has had enough, as mass celebrations repeatedly spiral into chaos..

entry.title: BYD is assuming financial liability if you crash while using its self-driving tech
entry.content: BYD is giving its self-driving tech a big vote of confidence.

entry.title: ‘I was getting ready to say goodbye’: cancer patient’s hope after smart drug success
entry.content: Pat Brogan preparing to walk his daughter down the aisle after trial of treatment designed to stop disease from hiding• Smart drug that strips cancer cells of ‘invisibility cloak’ can shrink tumours by 30%, trial showsOne of the first patients to benefit from a pioneering smart drug that appears to melt away the “invisibility cloak” that can shield cancer cells from treatment is Pat Brogan, from Cowdenbeath, Scotland.The 68-year-old, whose tumours have shrunk by almost a third, is preparing to walk his daughter down the aisle this month and holiday in Spain with his wife, Linda – milestones he once feared he would never reach. Continue reading...

entry.title: Smart drug that strips cancer cells of ‘invisibility cloak’ can shrink tumours by 30%, trial shows
entry.content: Experimental tablet produces encouraging results in patients with world’s most common forms of disease• ‘I was getting ready to say goodbye’: patient’s hope after smart drug successA smart drug that stops cancer cells “hiding” from treatment can shrink tumours by at least 30% in six of the world’s most common forms of the disease, early trial results show.While immunotherapy treatments have improved survival rates for many patients, their effectiveness can stall or fail when tumour cells hide and then spread. Continue reading...

entry.title: Add leaf blowers to the list of antisocial garden tools | Letters
entry.content: Miranda Fagandini says the noise made by these devices is dreadful and can be life-alteringOn reading the latest column in your long-running series (Lawnmower hum: why the sound of the summer could cost you £5,000, 27 May), I noticed that the writer didn’t include the curse of the leaf blower in their list of antisocial garden tools.The noise is dreadful and can be life-altering. We bought one which turned out to be so loud that it has caused permanent hearing loss and hyperacusis (sensitivity to loud noise) in my left ear. Continue reading...

entry.title: Florida sues OpenAI and Sam Altman over alleged 'exploitation of users'
entry.content: Florida's lawsuit against OpenAI comes after a mass shooter at FSU reportedly consulted ChatGPT to plan the attack.

entry.title: Seahawks could receive up to five 2027 compensatory draft picks
entry.content: The Seattle Seahawks could receive up to five 2027 compensatory draft picks after the latest departure.

entry.title: Doctors don’t know what to do about wellness influencers but we dismiss them at our peril | Ranjana Srivastava
entry.content: To be a cancer specialist is to see the worst of harm caused by social media. Yet I have never changed a patient’s mind with outrage“And so, of course, I have completely stopped eating red meat.”The “of course” is galling, especially since we have been using precious bags of blood to top up my patient’s haemoglobin.Ranjana Srivastava is an Australian oncologist, award-winning author and Fulbright scholar. Her latest book is Every Word Matters: Writing to Engage the Public Continue reading...

entry.title: Warriors predicted to beat out Lakers for $175 million superstar via four-team trade
entry.content: The Golden State Warriors would land a two-time MVP in a recent blockbuster trade proposal.

entry.title: 'This is where the monster was created': Caitlin Clark outburst at Iowa resurfaces amid Fever drama
entry.content: Fans will remember this over-the-top Clark moment.

entry.title: IPL 2027 mini-auction: Which players should CSK release after eighth-place finish under Ruturaj Gaikwad?
entry.content: Chennai Super Kings could go through some major changes in their squad ahead of the IPL 2027 season.

entry.title: Rams, Myles Garrett linked in potentially massive blockbuster trade
entry.content: Could this really happen? It appears to be in play.

entry.title: Why Tarik Skubal should want to be traded by Tigers this season
entry.content: This potential trade would bring some big benefits to Tarik Skubal.

entry.title: What Georgia baseball's manager said after controversial ejections
entry.content: Georgia Bulldogs baseball manager Wes Johnson gave his side of the story on the controversial Tre Phelps ejections

entry.title: T20 Mumbai 2026 tickets: Prices and how to buy tickets for season 4 matches at the Wankhede Stadium
entry.content: Get all details on T20 Mumbai 2026 tickets, including prices and how to book your seats for Season 4 matches at the Wankhede Stadium.

entry.title: Trump admirer’s surprise first-round win is a blow to Colombia’s traditional conservatives
entry.content: Success of far-right presidential candidate, Abelardo de la Espriella, suggests some voters are ‘fed up with politics’The far-right lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella and the leftwing senator Iván Cepeda have just under three weeks to compete for the roughly 3.6m votes that did not go to either of them in the first round of Colombia’s presidential election.That is no insignificant number, given that De la Espriella’s lead over Cepeda amounted to little more than 670,000 votes – 43.7% to 40.9%. Continue reading...

entry.title: Oil Extends Gains on Report Iran Is Halting Talks
entry.content: Crude futures extended gains on a report that Iran is halting talks with the U.S. through mediators in protest against Israeli attacks in Lebanon.

entry.title: Midwives want to make childbirth miraculous – so what went so wrong in Nottingham? | Zoe Williams
entry.content: The acronym ‘FOH’ for ‘Fuck off home’ was used beside the names of expectant mothers. Senior midwives advised others not to be ‘too kind’. But as this and other shocking evidence is brought to light, sexism is only one part of the storyIt’s said to be mother nature’s stunning con trick, the single most helpful move in the propagation of the species – that childbirth might be the worst thing ever to happen to anyone, but once you are through it, you instantly forget how painful it was. And that is true, up to a point, although you can often remember enough of the surrounding detail – swearing at strangers, wishing you were dead – that you can infer the rest.What you don’t forget, however, is what the midwives were like, and nor, even in moments of extremis, do you fail to notice if they’re treating you scornfully. Panorama tonight is about the maternity unit run by Nottingham university hospitals NHS trust, the subject of the largest maternity inquiry in NHS history, spanning 13 years from 2012, and covering 2,500 families. The details are hair-raising: “FOH” written next to women’s names on a whiteboard, which stood for “fuck off home”; accounts of senior midwives advising others not to be “too kind”; gut-wrenching individual cases of women being warned off coming in to hospital for so long that, when one finally arrived, her baby was dead and her perineum and vaginal wall had collapsed. And every one of those women will have known, on some level, even if she was in no state to ask for her notes or read them, that someone wanted her to “fuck off”. You get a superpower in a life-and-death situation, though it’s unclear how helpful it is: you can tell pretty fast who’s on your side and who isn’t. Continue reading...

entry.title: Yoga can reduce anxiety and insomnia for people living with cancer, study finds
entry.content: First clinical trial of its kind, involving 410 cancer survivors in US, also finds reductions in distress and fatigueYoga can reduce emotional distress, anxiety, fatigue and insomnia in people living with cancer, according to the results of the first clinical trial of its kind.Hundreds of millions of people worldwide are living with cancer, with advances in treatments meaning more patients are surviving the disease than ever before. But for many, the physical and mental side-effects of their diagnosis and treatment regime can last long after treatment ends. Continue reading...

entry.title: Berkshire buys Taylor Morrison for $6.8 billion. Buffett touts Abel’s dealmaking
entry.content: The acquisition marks one of the first major strategic deals under Warren Buffett's successor Greg Abel, who took over as CEO in the beginning of 2026.

entry.title: A robot is helping an ailing couple stay in their home. Are more to come for an aging population?
entry.content: The decades-long quest to build home robots that are both helpful and lifelike — spurred on by fictional machines like The Jetsons’ humanoid maid Rosie —- is still mostly a pipe dream, but some developers are getting closer

entry.title: ChatGPT Stock Portfolio: Is Progressive (PGR) The Best Stock to Buy in 2026?

entry.title: ASUS' ROG Xbox Ally X20 bundle includes a limited-edition OLED Ally X handheld PC and AR gaming glasses
entry.content: ASUS' new ROG Xbox Ally X20 has mostly the same hardware as last year's handheld, but now comes with a new OLED panel.

entry.title: To die with dignity: my young husband’s final wish came with a $65,000 price tag
entry.content: My husband, Craig, didn’t want to spend his last days in the hospital. His fight with bladder cancer then became a battle to get him hospice care at home“This isn’t where I want to die,” my husband, Craig, whispered to me.We were in a shared room on the top floor of NYU Langone hospital in Manhattan, the window obscured by a long privacy curtain. I barely had space to stand next to his hospital bed under the bright fluorescent lights, our thoughts interrupted by the constant beeping of machines. Continue reading...

entry.title: 10 Overvalued Stocks Are Ticking Time Bombs In Your Portfolio
entry.content: 10 Overvalued Stocks Are Ticking Time Bombs In Your Portfolio

entry.title: A cancer vaccine made just for you. mRNA is back and it's fighting melanoma
entry.content: A study finds that an mRNA vaccine is highly effective at preventing recurrence of this dangerous skin cancer, when used in combination with Keytruda, an immunotherapy drug.

entry.title: People ‘panicking’ as Ghana passes sweeping law criminalising LGBTQ+ activity
entry.content: Community groups say some fear they could lose homes, jobs and access to healthcare if the new law is ratified by President John Dramani MahamaGhana’s LGBTQ community is living in fear after the country’s parliament approved a sweeping bill that criminalises the promotion of LGBTQ+ activities and identifying as lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender or queer, rights groups have warned.The legislation, which was passed on Friday, mandates prison sentences of three to 10 years. Continue reading...

entry.title: Nvidia, Meta and Schlumberger rank among top companies adopting AI, new study says
entry.content: The AI-Driven Enterprise Institute released new research that breaks down how well S&P 500 companies are adopting AI compared to their peers.

entry.title: Vallejo fined for comments about female umpire
entry.content: Adolfo Daniel Vallejo is fined $65,000 (£48,300) for making "unacceptable" comments about a female umpire following his French Open exit.

entry.title: From Yeboah's shirt to scoring at 16
entry.content: James Milner was just 16 and earning £70 a week as a YTS player when he broke into the first team at Leeds, six months after taking his GCSE exams. On 10 November 2002, his dad Peter was in the away end at Upton Park when boss Terry Venables sent the youngster on for Jason Wilcox, now director of football at Manchester United, in the final minutes of a 4-3 win against West Ham. It was a dream come true for Milner, who had grown up supporting the club and wore shirts with the names of Tony Yeboah and Tony Dorigo on the back.

entry.title: Is Craig Breslow going to be in charge of the Red Sox for much longer?
entry.content: Well, that was a little bit better of a week for the Red Sox, I suppose? I mean, 3-3 still isn’t great or anything, but I feel slightly better on the heels of a weekend road victory. I guess The offense is coming around a bit (how sticky that is, who’s to say), guys like […]

entry.title: Football fans brawl after Champions League final
entry.content: Suffolk Police arrest two people in connection with a "large fight" that left one person injured.

entry.title: Goalkeeper Muric to leave Ipswich for Sassuolo
entry.content: Ipswich Town goalkeeper Arijanet Muric makes a permanent move to Serie A side Sassuolo after spending the season on loan in Italy.

entry.content: Reviewing the Detroit Lions' 2026 free agency class, including Cade Mays. What was the average age and medical concern level?

entry.title: Confirmed Ebola cases in Congo reach 282 as survivors describe their recoveries
entry.content: The outbreak remains focused in Congo's eastern Ituri province. Congo has reported over 1,000 suspected cases with the Bundibugyo virus, which has no approved treatment or vaccine.

entry.title: Trump says Iran "really wants" to make a deal, "will all work out well"
entry.content: Trump tells his critics to "sit back and relax" after another exchange of airstrikes tests the shaky U.S.-Iran ceasefire.

entry.title: UK will not have to pay Rwanda £100m over failed asylum scheme, court rules
entry.content: Rwanda had sued UK government over alleged breach of agreement, after scheme scrapped by Labour on first day in officeThe UK will not have to pay the Rwandan government millions of pounds over a failed migrant deportation scheme set up by Boris Johnson’s administration, an international court has ruled.The east African nation had sued the current UK government for more than £100m, claiming it was owed after a breach of an agreement. Continue reading...

entry.title: Suspected WWII bomb explodes in Indonesia, killing 5 people
entry.content: A suspected shell left over from World War II exploded under a house, killing five people and wounding nearly 20, police said Monday.

entry.title: Protesters, N.J. State Police clash near Delaney Hall, despite curfew
entry.content: Protesters and New Jersey State Police clashed again Sunday night near Delaney Hall, despite a 9 p.m. curfew imposed by Newark Mayor Ras Baraka.

entry.title: Colombia’s far-right presidential candidate De la Espriella wins first round of vote ahead of runoff
entry.content: Lawyer and Trump admirer has risen rapidly in the polls and will face Iván Cepeda in election runoff in three weeksThe far-right lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella won the first round of Colombia’s presidential election on Sunday and will face senator Iván Cepeda, the candidate backed by leftwing president Gustavo Petro, in the runoff.With 100% of ballots counted, the outsider and Donald Trump admirer De la Espriella secured 43.7% of the vote – just over 10.3m votes – compared with 40.9% (about 9.6m votes) for Cepeda, a philosopher and human rights activist who has served as a senator since 2014. Continue reading...

entry.title: Trump plan would allow for quick asylum rejections, documents show
entry.content: The regulation, described in internal documents obtained by CBS News, would be the latest effort by President Trump's White House to tighten access to the U.S. asylum system.

entry.title: Why It Matters if OpenAI or Anthropic Wins the IPO Race
entry.content: There is much to gain for the company that moves faster.

entry.title: A nurse stole fentanyl and AI missed it, state records say
entry.content: Sentri7, drug diversion software powered by artificial intelligence and used at hundreds of U.S. hospitals, did not catch a monthslong string of fentanyl thefts in Tennessee in 2025, according to a state document.

entry.title: Is bovine colostrum really 'liquid gold' for gut health?
entry.content: Baby calves rely on it to build up their immune systems and gut. And now marketers are promoting it for humans. Here's what scientists say.

entry.title: Primary challengers threaten to unseat House members across the country
entry.content: Four House members have already lost re-election bids so far this year, just a few months into primary season.

entry.title: How high-end credit card perks are hurting shoppers who pay in cash
entry.content: A Harvard study estimates that people who pay with cash and debit cards are subsidizing $30 billion a year in points and perks for credit card users.

entry.title: Senate Democrats launch campaign to kill DOJ's "anti-weaponization fund"
entry.content: Senate Democrats are launching a coordinated effort to kill the Trump administration's $1.7+ billion "anti-weaponization fund."

entry.title: Chuck Schumer lays out plan to fight Trump’s ‘anti-weaponization’ fund in the Senate
entry.content: The Senate minority leader said Democrats would use Senate procedure to try to kill President Donald Trump’s $1.8 billion “anti-weaponization” fund, forcing Republicans to vote to preserve the compensation money.

entry.title: U.S. Strikes Iranian Air Defenses, Drone Site in New Hormuz Skirmish

entry.title: Platner's wife calls news coverage of Senate hopeful's sexually explicit texts with women 'shameful'

entry.title: Newark mayor imposes curfew around Delaney Hall after clashes over immigration detention center
entry.content: The mayor of Newark has imposed a curfew around an immigration detention center in New Jersey after a series of intense clashes between protesters and police

entry.title: The AI Trade Hits Overdrive, Powering Stocks to Historic Gains
entry.content: The S&P 500 just posted one of its best two-month runs ever. That often means more good times ahead.

entry.title: Right-Wing Firebrand Surges Into Colombia Runoff, Shaking Political Establishment
entry.content: Abelardo de la Espriella, a rich lawyer who promised an iron-fisted crackdown on drug thugs, finished ahead of ruling movement’s leftist hopeful.

entry.title: Fortune Cookie of the Day
entry.content:
The easiest way to find a lost object is to buy a replacement.
Lucky Numbers: 44, 24, 10, 9, 7, 19

entry.title: Life Hack of the Day
entry.content: Use bread clips to organize your power cables.

entry.title: Useless Fact of the Day
entry.content: MTV first aired at 12:01 AM on August 1, 1981.

entry.title: Quote of the Day — Winston Churchill
entry.content: “Continuous effort - not strength or intelligence - is the key to unlocking our potential.” — Winston Churchill

entry.title: Shared NHS patient records could cut 20,000 A&E visits a year, ministers claim
entry.content: Modernisation bill would require GPs and hospitals in England to share data, reducing errors and duplicationSharing access to patients’ health data across NHS providers in England could result in 20,000 fewer A&E visits a year and save £20m annually, the government has claimed, before the second reading of the NHS modernisation bill on Monday.The bill, which would also abolish NHS England, sets out measures including single patient records (SPR) for every person receiving health and social care in England, requiring GPs and hospitals to securely share data as part of the government’s 10-year health plan. Continue reading...

entry.title: Here’s the biggest news you missed this weekend
entry.content: Family visitation at a New Jersey immigration detention center is set to resume after being suspended amid days of protests, arrests and clashes outside the facility, Gov.

entry.title: Berkshire Hathaway to Buy Homebuilder Taylor Morrison for $6.8 Billion
entry.content: The deal is one of the first big moves by Greg Abel, who succeeded Warren Buffett as chief executive officer in January.

entry.title: Hegseth Says U.S. Needs More From Asian Allies to Secure Balance Against China
entry.content: The defense secretary adopted a softer tone on Beijing in a speech to partners concerned about the U.S.’s security commitment.

entry.title: ‘Players require rest’: Gavaskar questions IPL 2026 final schedule, says GT deserved a fairer deal
entry.content: Sunil Gavaskar has criticized the IPL 2026 final's scheduling, suggesting a postponement due to Gujarat Titans' travel woes. Disruptions delayed their arrival in Ahmedabad, leaving them with less than 24 hours to prepare after an exhausting playoff run. Gavaskar believes a reserve day should have been utilized for a fairer contest.

entry.title: How much money did the Arizona Cardinals spend on UDFAs in 2026?
entry.content: A look at the contract details of the eight rookie free agents the Cardinals signed this year.

entry.title: How Can I Watch The NBA Finals? Schedule, Times And TV Channel
entry.content: The start time, TV channel, and watch guide for the NBA Finals featuring the New York Knicks and San Antonio Spurs. Game 1 is June 3 at 8:30 p.m. ET on ABC.
